description: Strategy registry for the experiment agent. Each entry is a strategy variant (code change or config preset) with its status and performance data.

strategies.description: Wide beam + moderate noise. Proven best for n=25 admission rate.
strategies.notes: Dominant config across local and production. 3-7x better than alternatives.

strategies.description: Focused edge flipping with moderate depth.
strategies.notes: Second-best. High discovery count but lower admission rate. Different exploration mode.
Underperforms wide-a. Too wide without enough noise = less diverse exploration.
Worst performer. Slow rounds, few discoveries.

strategies.description: Score-aware tabu walk within valid-graph space. Replaces 3-step greedy polish with 100-500 step tabu walk using incremental 4-clique and triangle deltas.

metrics.note: Enabled all workers to find rank-1 quality graphs. Core improvement.

strategies.notes: Highest-ROI code change. Directly targets scoring quality.

strategies.description: Cross-round fingerprint dedup persistence via carry_state.

metrics.note: Prevents re-exploration across rounds. ~15% unique discovery improvement.

strategies.description: Prefer balanced kc/ei in beam selection when violations are low.

metrics.note: Steers beam toward regions producing better-scoring valid graphs.

strategies.description: Iterated Local Search polish: chain multiple tabu walks separated by random valid-preserving perturbations. Escapes local optima that single walks converge to.
strategies.notes: Backward compatible (restarts=0 = current behavior). Recommended test: restarts=3, perturb=3 with polish_max_steps=100. Expected ~4x polish cost per valid graph but diverse score-space exploration.

strategies.description: Crossover recombination strategy: combines structural features from two parent graphs via uniform edge crossover, then repairs violations using tabu search. Designed to escape tree2's exhausted search space by exploring combinatorial blends of known-good graphs.
strategies.notes: Addresses tree2 exhaustion finding. Uses carry_state pool to accumulate valid graphs for future crossover parents. First round uses perturbed init_graph as parent B. Select via --strategy crossover. 5 new tests passing (R(3,3)/n=5, R(4,4)/n=17, observer, carry_state, pool reuse).

strategies.description: 2-opt paired edge flips in polish: when the tabu walk stalls (10 steps without best-score improvement), also evaluate pairs of edges that individually violate validity but combined preserve it. Expands the reachable neighborhood beyond single-flip connectivity to escape quality basins that exhaust single flips.
strategies.notes: Backward compatible (polish_2opt=false default). Enable via --polish-2opt=true. Overhead: ~2ms/step when stalled (vs ~10μs for 1-opt). Only activates after 10 steps without improvement. Addresses tree2+crossover exhaustion at 4c=(67,67) ceiling. 3 new tests passing (2-opt validity, ILS with 2-opt, zero-restarts equivalence). Available in tree2, crossover, and tabu strategies.

strategies.description: Simulated Annealing strategy: traverses through invalid states via probabilistic acceptance to escape the 4c>=67 basin. Composite objective (violations*W + max_4c*100 + min_4c) with linear cooling. At high temp, accepts moves introducing violations if they lower 4-clique counts. At low temp, converges to valid graphs in potentially different quality basins.
strategies.notes: Addresses exhaustion of all constrained methods (tree2, crossover, 2-opt) at 4c=(67,67). Key difference: SA can cross through 1-2 violation states to reach different valid-graph basins. Select via --strategy sa. 6 new tests (R(3,3)/n=5, R(4,4)/n=17, multiple discoveries, objective math). CLI flags: --sa-initial-temp, --sa-violation-weight. Max 5 polish calls per round.

strategies.description: Greedy construction strategy: builds R(k,l)-valid graphs from scratch via randomized edge-coloring + tabu repair, bypassing Paley-derived basin entirely. Each trial uses a different random edge ordering, producing structurally diverse graphs unreachable by perturbation from fixed seeds.
strategies.notes: Addresses the comprehensive 4c>=67 barrier. Key difference from all other strategies: does NOT use Paley/leaderboard seeds. Builds graphs edge-by-edge with greedy violation minimization, then tabu-repairs near-valid results. Select via --strategy construct. CLI flags: --repair-max-iters, --repair-threshold. 5 tests passing (R(3,3)/n=5, R(4,4)/n=17, observer, init_graph ignored, seed diversity). Polish integrated (capped at 20/round). Recommended fleet test: 4-8 workers with --strategy construct --repair-threshold 15 --repair-max-iters 20000 --max-iters 500000.

strategies.description: Circulant graph enumeration: exhaustively searches all 2^⌊n/2⌋ circulant graphs C(n, S) for R(k,ℓ) validity. Algebraically structured subspace completely different from Paley perturbation. Circulant graphs are vertex-transitive (|Aut| ≥ n) for direct scoring advantage. One-round exhaustive search (4096 graphs for n=25) with polish on valid discoveries.
strategies.notes: Fundamentally different approach: algebraic enumeration, not heuristic search. Uses carry_state to skip after first round. Select via --strategy circulant. 7 tests passing (R(3,3)/n=5, R(4,4)/n=17, build correctness x3, carry_state, dedup). Recommended test: single worker with --strategy circulant --n 25 --max-iters 1000000. Completes in one round (~seconds for validity check, longer if many valid graphs found and polished).

strategies.description: Large Neighborhood Search: exhaustive Gray code enumeration of all 2^m edge rewirings within random vertex blocks of size k. Explores distance-15 neighborhoods (k=6) vs radius-1 of all single-flip methods. For k=6: 32768 states per block at ~1ms. Complementary to all existing strategies — finds valid graphs unreachable by single, paired, or SA-based edge flips.
strategies.notes: Addresses structural 4c>=67 barrier by exploring multi-edge neighborhoods exhaustively. Select via --strategy lns. CLI flag: --lns-block-size (default 6, range 4-8). 5 tests passing (R(3,3)/n=5, R(4,4)/n=17, discovery reporting, budget respect, edge count). Recommended test: 4-8 workers with --strategy lns --lns-block-size 6 --max-iters 500000 --n 25. Block sizes: k=5 (1K states/block, many blocks/round), k=6 (32K states/block, ~15 blocks/round at 500K iters), k=7 (2M states/block, slow). Uses Paley graph fallback for cold start, leaderboard seeds in production.

strategies.description: Cayley Z_p×Z_p graph enumeration: exhaustively searches all 2^((p²-1)/2) Cayley graphs of the elementary abelian group Z_p×Z_p for n=p². Different algebraic family from circulant (Z_n). For n=25: 4096 candidates including the Paley graph P(25)≅Cay(GF(25),QR). All graphs are vertex-transitive (|Aut|≥25). One-round exhaustive search with polish on valid discoveries.
strategies.notes: Fundamentally different algebraic subspace from circulant strategy. Z_5×Z_5 ≠ Z_25 — different automorphism structure, different graph families. The Paley graph P(25) is a Cayley graph of GF(25)≅Z_5×Z_5 over quadratic residues, so this space contains it plus 4095 alternatives. Select via --strategy cayley. 11 tests passing (prime_sqrt, pair enumeration x3, build correctness x2, R(3,3)/n=4, R(4,4)/n=9, non-p² skip, carry_state x2). Recommended test: single worker with --strategy cayley --n 25. Completes in one round (~seconds). Supports n=4,9,25 (p=2,3,5); n=49 (p=7, 16M graphs) skipped as too large.

strategies.description: Path relinking: systematically walks between pairs of elite leaderboard graphs, discovering valid intermediates along each corridor. Accumulates a pool of diverse seeds over rounds, then relinks each pair (forward + backward). Allows brief traversal through 1-2 violation states to tunnel between basins. Fundamentally different from all local-search methods: guided moves toward a known-good target instead of random/greedy neighborhoods.
strategies.notes: Addresses the comprehensive 4c>=67 barrier via corridor exploration between known-good solutions. Select via --strategy relink. CLI flags: --pool-capacity (default 50), --max-violations (default 2). 6 tests passing (pool accumulation, two-graph start, valid intermediates, carry_state, budget, R(4,4)/n=17). Pool accumulates over first ~2 rounds, then starts relinking. Recommended test: 2-4 workers with --strategy relink --n 25 --max-violations 2 --pool-capacity 50. Needs several rounds to build pool from leaderboard seeds.

strategies.description: Two-phase gradient descent: Phase 1 greedily minimizes max(red_4c, blue_4c) via directed edge flips ignoring violations entirely, Phase 2 repairs violations via tabu search. Key difference from SA: directed moves (always picks BEST edge per step) rather than random moves with probabilistic acceptance. Phase separation ensures full-commitment 4c descent before repair, potentially reaching different validity basins.
strategies.notes: Addresses 4c>=67 barrier from a new angle: directed descent pushes max(4c) far below 67 (at cost of validity), then repair re-establishes validity possibly in a different basin. Select via --strategy gradient. CLI flags: --gradient-descent-steps, --gradient-repair-steps, --gradient-tabu-tenure, --gradient-perturb-flips. 4 tests passing (R(3,3)/n=5, R(4,4)/n=17, discovery reporting, multi-trial). Recommended test: 4-8 workers with --strategy gradient --n 25 --gradient-descent-steps 200 --gradient-repair-steps 5000 --max-iters 500000. Also try varied descent depths (50-500) and perturb_flips (3-10).

strategies.description: Seidel switching search: applies structured multi-edge perturbations (flipping ALL edges between a vertex subset S and V\S) to explore graph space algebraically. Two phases: exhaustive enumeration of small switching sets (size 1-3), then random switching compositions. Fundamentally different from all local-search methods — produces O(n) correlated edge changes at once.
strategies.notes: 11th strategy. Well-known operation in two-graph theory / Ramsey research. Switching preserves the switching equivalence class. For n=25: size 1-3 exhaustive = 2625 switchings per seed, plus 5000 random compositions. Select via --strategy seidel. CLI flags: --seidel-max-switch-size, --seidel-random-compositions. 8 tests passing (involution, edge changes, identity, R(3,3)/n=5, R(4,4)/n=17, no-init, discovery, random subset). Recommended test: 4-8 workers with --strategy seidel --n 25 --seidel-max-switch-size 3 --seidel-random-compositions 10000 --max-iters 500000.

ideas.description: Tune Seidel params: max_switch_size (2-5), random_compositions (1k-50k), and test with different seeds (top-1 vs random leaderboard). Also try size-4 exhaustive (12650 switchings per seed) and pure-random mode (no exhaustive phase, just compositions).
ideas.rationale: Seidel implemented but untested. Key tradeoff: larger switching sets produce more diverse graphs but are more likely to break validity. Compositions of two switchings explore deeper neighborhoods. Need to determine if Seidel-switched graphs can escape the 4c>=67 barrier.

ideas.description: Use valid Cayley Z_5×Z_5 graphs as alternative seeds for tree2 beam search. Different algebraic structure may lead to different 4-clique basins than Paley-perturbed seeds.
ideas.rationale: If cayley enumeration finds valid R(5,5) Cayley graphs with different 4-clique distributions, using them as tree2 seeds could escape the Paley basin. Requires cayley strategy to run first.

ideas.description: Tune LNS block_size (5 vs 6 vs 7) and max_iters (500k-2M). k=5 gives many small blocks (broad sampling), k=6 gives fewer large blocks (deep exploration). A/B test on n=25 production.
ideas.rationale: LNS implemented but untested in fleet. The tradeoff between block size and number of blocks explored is crucial. Also test whether LNS from top-scoring leaderboard seeds finds novel 4c<67 graphs.

ideas.description: Tune construct params: repair_threshold (5-30), repair_max_iters (5k-50k), max_iters (200k-2M). A/B test vs tree2-wide-a on n=25.
ideas.rationale: Construct implemented but untested in fleet. Need to find optimal repair tolerance and budget. Higher repair_threshold = more near-miss graphs repaired but slower per trial.

ideas.description: Tune SA params: initial_temp (5-50), violation_weight (5-50), max_iters (500k-2M). A/B test vs tree2-wide-a on n=25.
ideas.rationale: SA exhausted at n=25 4c>=67 barrier (73K rounds, temps 5-50, zero discoveries). Deprioritized.

ideas.description: Hybrid SA+tabu: use tabu list to prevent revisiting recently flipped edges during SA walk, improving exploration efficiency.
ideas.rationale: Pure random SA may revisit the same edges repeatedly. A short tabu list (tenure ~10-20) would force more diverse exploration without fundamentally changing the SA dynamics.

ideas.description: Dynamically adjust polish_max_steps based on how many valid graphs are found per round. More valid graphs = shorter polish per graph.
ideas.rationale: Currently polish depth is fixed. First round finds many valid graphs (slow), later rounds find few (wasted budget). Adaptive would balance.

ideas.description: Promote tabu search strategy to production fleet. Currently only tree2 is used in experiments.
ideas.rationale: Tabu explores depth (single trajectory with escape). Complementary to tree2's breadth. Already implemented but untested in fleet.

ideas.description: GPU-accelerated clique counting and candidate evaluation via CUDA or wgpu compute shaders. nvidia GPU available locally.
ideas.rationale: GPU NOT worthwhile for n=25 (violation_delta=33.6ns, GPU launch=5-10μs). Only revisit for n=40+.
ideas.estimated_effort: high — CUDA FFI or wgpu compute shader, significant engineering

ideas.description: Use valid circulant graphs found by circulant enumeration as alternative seeds for tree2 beam search. Different seed structure may lead to different 4-clique basins.
ideas.rationale: If circulant enumeration finds valid R(5,5) circulant graphs on n=25, they have fundamentally different structure from Paley-derived seeds. tree2 beam search from these seeds might explore different neighborhoods. Requires circulant strategy to find valid graphs first.

ideas.description: Run 16 workers simultaneously with known-best config. Pure throughput play.
ideas.rationale: On saturated leaderboards, more compute = more chances to find rare better graphs. Check CPU headroom first. Zero code changes needed.

ideas.description: Tune gradient params: descent_steps (50-500), repair_steps (2k-20k), perturb_flips (0-15), tabu_tenure (10-50). A/B test descent depth: shallow (50 steps, more trials/round) vs deep (500 steps, fewer trials but deeper 4c push). Also test with different seeds (top-1 vs random leaderboard).
ideas.rationale: Gradient implemented but untested in fleet. Key tradeoff: deeper descent reaches lower max(4c) but harder to repair back to validity. Shallow descent is closer to current valid-graph space. The sweet spot determines whether directed descent can escape the 4c>=67 basin that all random/constrained methods hit.

ideas.description: Tune relink params: pool_capacity (10-100), max_violations (0-5), and test with higher polish (200 steps). Compare pool growth rate and discovery quality vs tree2-wide-a.
ideas.rationale: Relink implemented but untested in fleet. Key tradeoff: max_violations=0 (strict validity) finds fewer intermediates but guarantees quality; max_violations=2-5 tunnels through invalid states for broader exploration. Pool needs ~5-10 rounds to saturate from leaderboard diversity.
